title: Observations on light hitting a concrete wall at 4PM.
excerpt: There is a specific quality to the light in late autumn. It doesn't just illuminate; it carves. The shadows are long, indigo-tinted, stretching across the floor like memories of the day just passed.

title: The Architecture of Stillness
excerpt: A study on how physical spaces dictate internal volume. If the room is cluttered, can the mind truly expand?

title: Winter Solitude
excerpt: A photographic meditation on the quiet beauty of empty winter landscapes and the solitude they invite.

title: On the Impermanence of Sound
excerpt: Sound exists only in the moment of its creation. Unlike light, it cannot be captured — only approximated.

title: A Room Without Furniture
excerpt: What does an empty room communicate? The absence of objects speaks louder than their presence.
